messages.content: You are BioGPT, an expert compiler architect. Translate the provided natural language biological protocol into valid, deterministic BioScript code for execution on a microfluidic biochip.

Perform a direct competitive ELISA for morphine detection in urine. Dispense 20 units of the urine sample into the enzyme-coated well and mix. Dispense 100 units of morphine conjugate into the well, mix for 60 seconds, and incubate at 23C for 60 minutes. After incubation, dispose of the reaction liquids. Perform a wash cycle by repeating the following steps 6 times: dispense 350 units of distilled water into the well, mix for 45 seconds, and dispose of the wash water. To develop the color, dispense 100 units of TMB substrate into the well, mix, and incubate at 23C for 30 minutes. Halt the reaction by dispensing 100 units of stop reagent and mixing for 60 seconds. Finally, detect fluorescence on the completed assay for 30 minutes before final disposal.

module fluorescence

manifest morphine_enzyme

manifest urine_sample
manifest morphine_conjugate
manifest distilled_water
manifest tmb_substrate
manifest morphine_enzyme
manifest stop_reagent

instructions:

e = dispense 20 units of urine_sample
f = dispense morphine_enzyme
a = mix e with f
g = dispense 100 units of morphine_conjugate
h = dispense morphine_enzyme
b = mix g with h for 60s
heat b at 23c for 60m
dispose a
dispose b

repeat 6 times {
    i = dispense 350 units of distilled_water
    j = dispense morphine_enzyme
    b = mix i with j for 45s
    dispose b
}

k = dispense 100 units of tmb_substrate
l = dispense morphine_enzyme
cc = mix k with l
heat cc at 23c for 30m
m = dispense 100 units of stop_reagent
d = mix cc with m for 60s

urine_reading = detect fluorescence on d for 30m
dispose d

metadata.notes: IR Validated. Standard 6-wash loop successfully maps to the BioScript DAG. Uses morphine_enzyme as the persistent fluid representing the microtiter well.
